objective: Add 5 structured proposal types (feature, security, ux, release, policy) so the DAO only accepts typed proposals. Agents adapt their analysis per type.

content: Use StringEnum from @mariozechner/pi-ai for the type parameter in dao_propose tool (Google API compatibility)
reason: Pi uses StringEnum instead of Type.Union/Type.Literal for compatibility with Google API providers

content: Security proposals will have security-review gate promoted to blocker severity
reason: Security proposals need stricter quality gates — the security-review gate should block execution if not passed

content: All 7 agent prompts will include type-specific guidance section
reason: Agents should adapt their analysis based on proposal type (e.g., Critic more severe on security proposals)
No visual design needed, pure code feature — skip DESIGN phase, delegate to @codegen

issues.description: No backward-compatibility migration — proposals restored from persistence without a 'type' field will crash or render 'undefined' in TYPE_EMOJI[p.type], PROPOSAL_TYPE_LABELS[p.type], and formatProposalPrompt(). Add default fallback in restoreState().
buildExecutionPrompt() does not include proposal type — delivery agent cannot apply its type adaptation guidance during execution. Add type to the execution prompt header.
Line 766: selectedType cast as inline union duplicate ('feature' | 'security' | ...) instead of using ProposalType. Use 'as ProposalType' for DRY.
Line 230: params.type as import('./types.js').ProposalType is a type assertion masking the StringEnum output. Add a comment explaining why the cast is safe.

Spec says 'security-review gate promoted to blocker' but implementation promotes 'risk-threshold' gate. Gate 'security-review' exists only as a checklist item, not a quality gate. Spec should be updated to match implementation.
Line 5: 'ProposalType' imported but never directly used — only accessed through Proposal.type. Remove unused import.
TYPE_EMOJI duplicates emojis from PROPOSAL_TYPE_LABELS in types.ts. Two sources of truth for the same mapping. Consider deriving TYPE_EMOJI from PROPOSAL_TYPE_LABELS.

description: Added backward-compat migration: proposals without type default to 'feature'
Added proposal type to buildExecutionPrompt() header
Import ProposalType, use 'as ProposalType' casts with comment
Replaced duplicate TYPE_EMOJI with typeEmoji() derived from PROPOSAL_TYPE_LABELS
Fixed gate promotion spec to match implementation, added artifacts table

reason: All 7 review issues fixed, TypeScript compiles cleanly
